payload.description: A planning study of a citywide BRT network initiated in fall 2008 by DOT and NYCT, identifying 16 potential future BRT corridors based on transit needs analysis and public outreach including 7 public workshops and an online survey.
Potential BRT Phase II corridor in the Bronx; identified as a short-list candidate for the next BRT planning effort in NYC.
Potential BRT Phase II corridor in Queens; identified as a short-list candidate for the next BRT planning effort in NYC.
Potential BRT Phase II corridor in Queens comprised of several inter-related travel markets; requires separate alternatives analysis and coordination with Port Authority of NY and NJ.

payload.description: NYC DOT and MTA NYCT initiated a planning study of a citywide BRT network.

tool_args.label: Introduction to BRT Phase II report released

payload.description: Results of the transit needs analysis summarized in 'Introduction to Bus Rapid Transit Phase II' report.
Seven public workshops across the city and an online survey engaging the public on BRT. Over 325 people participated in workshops; over 600 completed the online survey.

tool_args.label: BRT Phase II Public Input report released

payload.description: 'Bus Rapid Transit Phase II: Public Input' report released summarizing results of public outreach.

payload.claim_text: NYCT and DOT have identified 16 potential future corridors for BRT Phase II based on transit needs analysis and public feedback.
payload.subject: BRT Phase II Study

payload.claim_text: NYCT and DOT evaluated over 40 potential BRT corridors on five primary criteria: community feedback, ridership potential, network benefits (connections to subway lines and other BRT routes), bus lane and busway feasibility, and geographic diversity.
payload.subject: BRT Phase II Study

payload.claim_text: NYCT and DOT identified a short list of three candidates for the next BRT planning effort: Webster Avenue/Third Avenue in the Bronx, Hillside Avenue in Queens, and LaGuardia/East Elmhurst in Queens.
payload.subject: BRT Phase II Study

tool_args.label: Community Advisory Committee for each corridor

payload.claim_text: For each corridor, NYCT and DOT will create a Community Advisory Committee, which will meet periodically during the planning for the corridor, as well as hold a series of open public workshops.
payload.subject: BRT Phase II Study

payload.raw_value_text: Over 325 people participated in the workshops
payload.description: Participants in seven public BRT workshops across NYC in spring 2009.

payload.raw_value_text: over 600 people completed the online survey
payload.description: Respondents to BRT online survey in spring 2009.

payload.raw_value_text: representatives from 47 community and business groups
payload.description: Community and business groups represented at BRT public workshops.

payload.raw_value_text: staff members from the offices of 23 local elected officials
payload.description: Local elected officials' offices with staff at BRT public workshops.
Initial list of potential BRT corridors compiled from transit needs analysis and public feedback.
